feed.title: unique_ptr<T> </T>
feed.subtitle: Write an awesome description for your new site here. You can edit this line in _config.yml. It will appear in your document head meta (for Google search results) and in your feed.xml site description.

entry.title: Welcome to Jekyll!
entry.content:
You’ll find this post in your _posts directory. Go ahead and edit it and re-build the site to see your changes. You can rebuild the site in many different ways, but the most common way is to run jekyll serve, which launches a web server and auto-regenerates your site when a file is updated.

To add new posts, simply add a file in the _posts directory that follows the convention YYYY-MM-DD-name-of-post.ext and includes the necessary front matter. Take a look at the source for this post to get an idea about how it works.

Jekyll also offers powerful support for code snippets:

Check out the Jekyll docs for more info on how to get the most out of Jekyll. File all bugs/feature requests at Jekyll’s GitHub repo. If you have questions, you can ask them on Jekyll Talk.
entry.summary: You’ll find this post in your _posts directory. Go ahead and edit it and re-build the site to see your changes. You can rebuild the site in many different ways, but the most common way is to run jekyll serve, which launches a web server and auto-regenerates your site when a file is updated.

entry.title: Claims Identity V2 Drop 2 Ready for You

link.title: Claims Identity V2 Drop 2 Ready for You

entry.content:
As normal I am late to the party. We have been working hard on getting the Claims Identity Guide updated for a V2. Our big focus for this drop is Windows Azure Access Control Service (ACS). We have Federation, SOAP, and Rest services. We have passive clients (browser) and Active clients (WPF & Windows Phone 7).

Eugenio has a couple of great write ups for this release you should check out:

  • Drop #2 of Claims Identity Guide on CodePlex
  • Authentication in WP7 client with REST Services–Part I

Another cool project to check out is the Windows Azure tools for Windows Phone 7.  Right now I am working on two things that  I should have out soon:

  • Multi Entity Schema storage
  • ACS and iPhone

Happy coding!
entry.summary: As normal I am late to the party. We have been working hard on getting the Claims Identity Guide updated for a V2. Our big focus for this drop is Windows Azure Access Control Service (ACS). We have Federation, SOAP, and Rest services. We have passive clients (browser) and Active clients (WPF & Windows Phone 7).

entry.title: Another Update for the Deployment scripts for Windows Azure

link.title: Another Update for the Deployment scripts for Windows Azure

entry.content:
Tom Hollander called me the other day looking for some advice on deployment scripts for Windows Azure. Given that I have not got around to updating these for the new SDK and commandlets, I decided it was about time. Tom did beat me to the punch and put out his own post (which is very good), yet I figured I would update the scripts and push them out to github.  Now anytime you want, you can fork, send me pull requests etc.

The only major changes besides the SDK and Commandlet updates where the ideas incorporated from Tom’s post. I now pass all the info to the PowerShell scripts from the msbuild file. I also fixed a problem in the deploy PowerShell script. If you don’t have the names of your service host and storage account the same, the deploy fails.  You can read more about it here.

Have fun in the cloud!
entry.summary: Tom Hollander called me the other day looking for some advice on deployment scripts for Windows Azure. Given that I have not got around to updating these for the new SDK and commandlets, I decided it was about time. Tom did beat me to the punch and put out his own post (which is very good), yet I figured I would update the scripts and push them out to github. Now anytime you want, you can fork, send me pull requests etc.

entry.title: A Survival Guide to Scrum
entry.content: My friend Mitch Lacy has been working on a book and is looking for feedback. If you ever wanted a survival guide to scrum, this is it. Go read, give feedback and enjoy!
entry.summary: My friend Mitch Lacy has been working on a book and is looking for feedback. If you ever wanted a survival guide to scrum, this is it. Go read, give feedback and enjoy!

entry.title: Come work with us at p&p

link.title: Come work with us at p&p

entry.content:
Ever wanted to change the world? You too could come and enjoy the fun we have every day. If you think you would like to do this go apply here.

SDE at p&p

Bob is our new fearless leader. Read more about what we did this year.
entry.summary: Ever wanted to change the world? You too could come and enjoy the fun we have every day. If you think you would like to do this go apply here.

entry.title: Claims Based Identity with Windows Azure Access Control Service (ACS)

link.title: Claims Based Identity with Windows Azure Access Control Service (ACS)

entry.content:
We are making good headway on our new project.  We have started converting our current scenarios over to ACS.  Eugenio has a couple of great posts on our first scenario using ACS as a Federation Provider.

  • Access Control Service as a Federation Provider
  • ACS as a Federation Provider - A little bit deeper into the sample (Home Realm Discovery)

As always go over to the Claims Identity codeplex site and provide feedback.  We are hoping to have our first drop the first of next week.
entry.summary: We are making good headway on our new project. We have started converting our current scenarios over to ACS. Eugenio has a couple of great posts on our first scenario using ACS as a Federation Provider.

entry.title: Claims Based Identity and Access Control

link.title: Claims Based Identity and Access Control

entry.content:
Over the past year we have shipped a few guides:

I didn’t participate in the first version of the guide, yet I learned more than my fair share doing the last 3 guides. This version (2) will update the current content as well add Windows Azure Access Control Service & claims enabling SharePoint. I will not repeat it here, so if you want to know all the gory details visit Eugenio’s Blog Post.
entry.summary: Over the past year we have shipped a few guides:

entry.title: Windows Azure & Windows Phone 7 Hands On Labs Available

link.title: Windows Azure & Windows Phone 7 Hands On Labs Available

entry.content:
After all the work we have done to ship the last 3 guides, we decided to go the extra mile and create some Hands On Labs (HOL) for each of the guides. These HOLs will guide you through development of the key features of the apps distributed with the books. Go get them today and learn even more about building Windows Azure and Windows Phone 7 applications.

  • Moving Applications to the Cloud
  • Developing Applications for the Cloud
  • Windows Phone 7 Developer Guide

Feed back always welcome.

Note

These HOLs are using the Windows Azure 1.2 SDK.  We are working on updating the content and HOLs to the Windows Azure 1.3 SDK.  These will be available on CodePlex as soon as we get them done.
entry.summary: After all the work we have done to ship the last 3 guides, we decided to go the extra mile and create some Hands On Labs (HOL) for each of the guides. These HOLs will guide you through development of the key features of the apps distributed with the books. Go get them today and learn even more about building Windows Azure and Windows Phone 7 applications.

entry.title: Windows Phone 7 Developer Guidance
entry.content:
Yesterday we released our guidance on Windows Phone 7.  This is a continuation of our Windows Azure Guidance that we did previously.  We took the survey application and  made a version for Windows Phone 7.  This guidance is a little more advance, yet we tried to make it very approachable.  Here are a few topics you will find in the guide:

  • Prism for Windows Phone 7
  • Reactive Extensions
  • WCF Services on top of Windows Azure
  • Push Notifications
  • Camera & Voice
  • Panorama
  • Much more…

I am proud of the work we have done and we had an amazing team once again.  Hope you guys enjoy it as much as we enjoyed building it.
entry.summary: Yesterday we released our guidance on Windows Phone 7. This is a continuation of our Windows Azure Guidance that we did previously. We took the survey application and made a version for Windows Phone 7. This guidance is a little more advance, yet we tried to make it very approachable. Here are a few topics you will find in the guide:

entry.title: Enterprise Library for Silverlight
entry.content:
Grigori posted the new backlog for Enterprise Library for Silverlight (so many found memories of Enterprise Library).  Now you can vote for stories you would like to have in this edition.  Your vote counts, but only if you vote.

Vote Now!
entry.summary: Grigori posted the new backlog for Enterprise Library for Silverlight (so many found memories of Enterprise Library). Now you can vote for stories you would like to have in this edition. Your vote counts, but only if you vote.
